front: Pub/Sub guarantees at-least-once delivery. How do you design consumers to handle duplicate messages?
back: At-least-once means a message may be delivered more than once, especially on subscriber restarts or network retries. Consumers must be idempotent: processing the same message twice should produce the same result. Common patterns: use the message_id as a deduplication key in a database upsert, track processed IDs in Memorystore or Bigtable, or design downstream writes as insert-or-ignore. Exactly-once semantics require application-level dedup; Pub/Sub itself does not guarantee it.
